dossiers.business: Willis Towers Watson (WTW) is a global advisory, broking, and solutions firm serving clients in risk management, insurance, and human capital. The company operates through two segments: Health, Wealth & Career (HWC), which provides benefits consulting and actuarial services, and Risk & Broking (R&B), which offers commercial risk brokerage. WTW generates the vast majority of revenue from recurring professional services and commissions across 140 countries. Following the failed Aon merger, management refocused on organic growth and margin expansion under its 'Accelerate' transformation plan.
dossiers.thesis: WTW sits at the intersection of insurance brokerage and human capital consulting, two stable, high-retention markets with pricing power. The Accelerate program targets ~$450M in annualized savings by 2027 while revenue grows mid-single digits organically. Insider buying and Pershing Square's position validate the thesis that the market undervalues post-transformation FCF power — we estimate $1.3B+ FCF in FY27 against a $13B market cap. The 2-year CAGR to fair value is attractive at current prices.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Copart operates the world's largest online vehicle auction marketplace, specializing in salvage and used vehicles for insurance companies, dealers, fleet operators, and individual sellers. It processes over 5 million vehicles per year globally across 250+ locations. The model is asset-light (sellers retain title) but capital-intensive for land storage. Revenue streams include auction fees, titling, transport, and storage. International expansion (UK, Germany, Finland, Middle East) diversifies the primarily US P&C insurance-driven business.
dossiers.thesis: Copart possesses a near-monopoly two-sided marketplace with multi-decade switching costs: insurers need the global buyer pool; buyers need the seller volume. Newer vehicles becoming total losses (more sensors/ADAS) structurally increases salvage rates. Management (Willis Johnson family) has compounded book value at >20% for 30 years with zero debt and high ROIC. At 30x forward FCF the stock looks fairly valued, but the quality and compounding make it a core long-term hold with reasonable upside if international achieves US margins.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Moody's Corporation is a global integrated risk assessment firm operating through two segments: Moody's Investors Service (MIS), which rates debt issuances, and Moody's Analytics (MA), which sells credit data, analytics, and risk software via subscription. MIS revenue is tied to debt capital market volumes; MA provides recurring SaaS-like revenue. Moody's is one of only three globally recognized rating agencies with an SEC-designated NRSRO status, creating regulatory barriers to entry that are effectively impossible to replicate.
dossiers.thesis: Moody's benefits from a regulatory-granted oligopoly in credit ratings with ~40% market share. MIS revenue is cyclical with debt issuance volumes, but Moody's Analytics (now ~55% of revenue) provides durable subscription growth at 9–11% annually. The secular growth of private credit, ESG ratings, and climate risk assessment expands TAM. At 35x forward EPS, valuation is full but justified by ROIC >100%, consistent FCF generation, and long runway for Analytics pricing power.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: EVERTEC is a payment technology and services company primarily serving financial institutions, merchants, and governments in Latin America and the Caribbean. Its ATH payment network is the dominant interbank network in Puerto Rico with ~90% market share, providing a monopoly-like position. The company processes over 2.5 billion transactions annually. It is expanding across Latin America through acquisitions and organic growth, targeting Brazil, Chile, Colombia, and Mexico.
dossiers.thesis: EVERTEC's ATH network in Puerto Rico is an irreplaceable piece of financial infrastructure — banks cannot route transactions without it, giving EVERTEC captive recurring revenue with near-zero churn. The company is early in monetizing its LatAm expansion playbook, with financial inclusion tailwinds driving digital payment penetration from low bases. Insider accumulation signals management confidence. At ~13x EBITDA vs. fintech peers at 20-25x, EVTC screens cheap for the quality of its Puerto Rico franchise.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Insulet Corporation makes the OmniPod insulin delivery system, a tubeless patch pump worn on the body for up to 3 days. Unlike traditional insulin pumps, OmniPod has no tubing and connects wirelessly to continuous glucose monitors (CGMs). It serves Type 1 and Type 2 diabetics. The US market generates ~40% of revenue; international (Europe, rest of world) represents the majority and is growing faster. The OmniPod 5 AID system received FDA clearance and is driving meaningful share gains in the automated insulin delivery segment.
dossiers.thesis: OmniPod's tubeless design is a durable differentiator — patients prefer it strongly over tubed pumps, creating high retention once initiated. The GLP-1 wave expands the addressable Type 2 population over time as GLP-1 users still need insulin titration. International penetration of insulin pumps is less than 20% of eligible patients vs. ~40% in the US, implying multi-year runway. Insider buying and a recent pullback from highs create an asymmetric setup — execution on OmniPod 5 uptake and international reimbursement wins are the key catalysts.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: TKO Group Holdings operates two premier sports entertainment brands: UFC (Ultimate Fighting Championship), the world's leading MMA promotion, and WWE (World Wrestling Entertainment), the global leader in professional wrestling. Both generate revenue from media rights, live events, sponsorships, and licensing. UFC media rights (ESPN deal worth $1.5B/yr through 2025) are being renegotiated at significantly higher rates. WWE's flagship Smackdown moved to Netflix in 2025 under a reported $5B deal. Endeavor's controlling stake means capital allocation is disciplined.
dossiers.thesis: TKO owns two of the world's scarcest live sports media rights. UFC media rights renewal (negotiated in 2025-26) should deliver a 3-4x increase based on comparable sports rights escalation. WWE's Netflix deal establishes a streaming royalty that grows with subscriber base. Neither brand requires massive capital investment — the content is intrinsically valued by a global fanbase. Insider buying signals management conviction. The combined entity can grow EBITDA 15-20% annually for multiple years on rights cycle tailwinds alone.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Mastercard operates a global four-party payment network connecting consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and governments across 210+ countries. It earns transaction fees (domestic and cross-border) plus value-added services (fraud management, cybersecurity, data analytics). The company does not take credit risk. Cross-border transactions are the highest-margin volume and recovered strongly post-COVID. Mastercard's Services segment (Verizon, NuData, Brighterion) generates ~30% of revenue at higher margins.
dossiers.thesis: Mastercard is an extraordinary business: it earns a toll on global commerce with minimal capital. Only Visa competes at true global scale. The secular shift from cash/check to digital payments has a long runway — over half of global transactions are still cash. Cross-border volumes will grow as travel rebounds. Services provide high-margin growth above the network toll. At ~35x forward PE, the multiple reflects quality; total return of 10-15% annually is driven by high-teens EPS growth plus buybacks. Berkshire and other smart-money holders anchor the thesis.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Zoetis is the world's largest company dedicated to animal health medicines and vaccines, serving livestock and companion animal markets in 100+ countries. Companion animals (dogs, cats) now represent ~60% of revenue and are growing faster than livestock. Zoetis has the broadest product portfolio in the industry, including blockbusters Apoquel (dermatology), Cytopoint, and a growing monoclonal antibody pipeline. The company spun out of Pfizer in 2013 and has since substantially expanded margins and returned capital.
dossiers.thesis: Pet humanization is a multi-decade trend — US pet spending grew 6% annually for the past decade and shows no sign of reversal. Zoetis captures this via recurring spend on prescription drugs (Apoquel/Cytopoint for allergies) and vaccines. New species-specific monoclonal antibodies (Librela for canine OA pain) are early in adoption with large untapped markets. International markets are underpenetrated vs. the US. Insider buying at current levels is notable given the stock's pullback from $180+ highs, suggesting management sees the stock as undervalued at 23x forward EPS.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Jack Henry & Associates provides technology solutions and payment processing services exclusively to US community and mid-tier banks and credit unions. Its core banking systems (Silverlake, CIF 20/20, Banno) run on long-term contracts with high switching costs — migration risk is existential for a bank. Revenue is ~85% recurring (processing, maintenance, SaaS). The company serves over 7,500 financial institutions and has a revenue run rate near $2.4B. Jack Henry's fiscal year ends in June.
dossiers.thesis: Jack Henry occupies a defensible niche: community banks cannot easily migrate away from deeply integrated core systems, locking in revenue for decades. The secular adoption of digital banking, real-time payments (FedNow), and cloud migration drives incremental spend per client. Competitive moat is institutional — Jack Henry's bank-only focus makes it more trusted than diversified fintech competitors. Insider purchases at current price levels signal management's conviction. Returns of 10-12% annually are driven by 8-10% revenue growth plus capital returns.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Installed Building Products (IBP) is the second-largest US installer of insulation and complementary building products (gutters, garage doors, fireproofing) in new residential and commercial construction. It operates through a branch-based model with over 220 locations. IBP has grown through a disciplined acquisition strategy of regional installers, consolidating a fragmented market. Residential housing accounts for ~75% of revenue; commercial and industrial make up the remainder.
dossiers.thesis: IBP sits at the intersection of two durable trends: US housing undersupply requiring 1-1.5M new units annually to clear the deficit, and energy efficiency mandates requiring higher insulation R-values in new construction. As a labor-based service, IBP benefits from pricing power driven by labor scarcity. The roll-up strategy compounds at high returns as small regional installers are acquired at 5-7x EBITDA. Insider buying reinforces conviction in the business. A 2-year horizon captures both the housing recovery and margin normalization.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: CAVA Group operates and licenses Mediterranean-inspired fast-casual restaurants, featuring build-your-own bowls, pitas, and salads. As of Q1 2026 the chain has approximately 400 locations, primarily in the US. The model is similar to Chipotle: a simple, customizable menu with a single kitchen production system. CAVA went public in 2023 at $22/share and has delivered strong same-restaurant sales growth. The company targets 1,000+ US locations over the next decade.
dossiers.thesis: CAVA is in the early innings of what could be a Chipotle-scale expansion story. Mediterranean is a differentiated cuisine category with broad appeal and healthier positioning. Same-restaurant sales comps have been exceptional (7-10%+), suggesting genuine demand rather than novelty. Insider purchases by founder and CEO Brett Schulman at current levels are a strong positive signal. The risk is execution — new restaurant economics must hold as expansion moves beyond core markets. At 80x EBITDA, valuation requires flawless execution for base-case returns.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Roper Technologies is a diversified software and engineered products company operating in healthcare IT, infrastructure software, and industrial solutions. It has reoriented its portfolio over the past decade toward high-margin, recurring software businesses through disciplined acquisitions (Vertafore, Frontline Education, PowerPlan, Aderant). Roper's software businesses serve niche verticals with high switching costs; it sells engineered products where performance, not price, drives the purchase. ROIC regularly exceeds 15%.
dossiers.thesis: Roper is a capital-efficient compounder — it acquires mission-critical vertical software businesses at mid-teens EBITDA multiples, applies operating discipline, and holds forever. The portfolio generates $2B+ in annual FCF, which is systematically redeployed into the next acquisition. Management's track record of value-creative M&A spanning 20+ years is unmatched among diversified technology companies. At 25x forward EPS, Roper is modestly cheap for the quality; smart-money backing confirms the thesis.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Fiserv is a global financial technology company providing payment processing, merchant acquiring (Clover POS), and banking technology to financial institutions. It is one of the world's largest payment technology companies by revenue, processing over 12 billion transactions annually. The 2019 acquisition of First Data integrated Clover (small business POS) into the portfolio. Revenue is weighted toward the Merchant Acceptance segment (Clover) and Financial Technology (core banking, payment networks for banks and credit unions).
dossiers.thesis: Fiserv's Clover POS platform is growing >20% annually by volume and has become a legitimate competitor to Square/Stripe in the small-to-medium business market. The banking technology segment has inherent retention (banks rarely change core processors) and benefits from a secular shift to real-time payments. The stock trades at ~15x forward EPS, a discount to payment peers, which reflects lingering integration skepticism from the First Data deal that has already been resolved. Insider buying signals management confidence in the Clover trajectory and FCF ramp.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: GE HealthCare Technologies is a global medical technology company spun off from General Electric in January 2023. It operates in four segments: Imaging (CT, MRI, X-ray), Ultrasound, Patient Care Solutions, and Pharmaceutical Diagnostics. It has a $20B+ revenue base and serves hospitals and healthcare systems globally. The imaging business is a global duopoly with Siemens Healthineers. AI-enhanced imaging workflows (Edison platform) are being integrated across the portfolio.
dossiers.thesis: As a newly independent company, GEHC is executing a classic spin-off playbook: shedding GE overhead, realigning incentives, and investing in higher-margin recurring services and software. The imaging installed base generates recurring service and upgrade revenue; AI-assisted workflows command premium pricing. Margin expansion from mid-teens EBIT to high-teens is achievable over 2-3 years as GE legacy costs roll off. Insider buying validates the transformation thesis. Valuation at ~20x forward EPS lags medical tech peers (30x+), representing a re-rating opportunity.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Boston Scientific is a global medical device company focused on cardiovascular intervention, electrophysiology, rhythm management, urology, and endoscopy. Its key growth products include WATCHMAN FLX (LAA closure for A-fib), FARAPULSE pulsed-field ablation catheters, and its neuromodulation portfolio. Revenue exceeds $16B annually across 130+ countries. The company has a track record of successful product launches and bolt-on acquisitions that expand addressable markets.
dossiers.thesis: BSX is in a product super-cycle: FARAPULSE is taking rapid share in the ablation market (A-fib treatment), WATCHMAN FLX penetration of the LAA closure market is less than 10% of eligible patients, and its electrophysiology mapping platform is increasingly best-in-class. These three drivers alone can sustain double-digit revenue growth for 3-5 years. Insider buying reinforces management's confidence. At ~28x forward EPS, BSX is fairly priced for a high-quality medtech compounder, with upside if FARAPULSE ramps faster than consensus expects.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: RLI Corp is a specialty insurance holding company focused on underwriting niche commercial lines where pricing discipline and expertise create sustainable profits. It operates in three segments: Casualty (excess & surplus, professional liability), Property (marine, commercial), and Surety. RLI has achieved a combined ratio below 100% for 29 consecutive years — one of the best records in the industry. The company is conservatively capitalized and targets 10-15% ROE through the cycle.
dossiers.thesis: RLI's 29-year underwriting profit streak is the best evidence of durable culture and risk selection discipline in specialty insurance. Unlike diversified insurers, RLI chooses only markets where it has an underwriting edge and exits when markets harden against it. The company benefits from rising rates in E&S lines following several CAT loss years. Insider buying at current price levels signals management views the stock as cheap relative to intrinsic value, which we estimate at ~1.5x book or $75-85 per share, implying 30-50% upside from here.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Meta Platforms operates Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger, reaching 3.5+ billion daily active people. The company earns primarily through targeted digital advertising across its apps. Reality Labs (VR/AR hardware and software) is a second segment investing heavily in the metaverse, currently generating losses of ~$15B annually. Meta's AI investments include Llama open-source models, AI Assistants within its apps, and infrastructure for ad-targeting. The company has $70B+ in cash and generates ~$50B in annual FCF.
dossiers.thesis: Meta's advertising business is the most efficient large-scale digital ad platform in the world — AI-driven Advantage+ has re-accelerated ROAS for advertisers following the ATT headwinds of 2021-22. The platform's scale (3.5B DAPs) is impossible to replicate. AI Assistants and WhatsApp monetization are incremental revenue layers not yet in numbers. Threads is growing as a X/Twitter alternative. At 25x forward EPS with 15-20% EPS growth, META is modestly priced for the quality. Smart-money positioning from multiple major value funds validates the thesis.
dossiers.author: oracle
